content.question: Whether individual-level SES gaps could have been closed by longer-duration exposure to the 1999 reform (the Polish cohort was measured at age 15, not adulthood) or whether structural timing reform alone is insufficient without targeted SES-based support programs remains untested.
content.reduction_path: Longitudinal follow-up of Polish cohorts into adulthood (age 25–35), measuring lifetime earnings, intergenerational mobility, and SES-conditional achievement by school-sector composition; similar cohort studies in other countries with delayed-tracking reforms.
